meaning: vt.预期,期望,过早使用,先人一着,占先v.预订,预见,可以预料
examples: We anticipate that sales will rise next year.
They anticipate moving to bigger premises by the end of the year.
Try to anticipate what your child will do and forestall problems.
Only 22% of them anticipate a decrease in hiring, down from 86% a year ago.
It will commission a panel to look into why we failed to anticipate the threat.
